text: use your best judgement and create some fieldsets (some hidden with 'classes': ('collapse',)) that group relevant information together and show the most important on top. ```py cloud/cloud/interests/admin.py from django.contrib import admin from django.urls import reverse from django.utils.safestring import mark_safe from cloud.interests.models import ( Calculation, File, Interest, Offer, PackagePrice, ShoppingCartPackage, UserInput, ) class CalculationInline(admin.StackedInline): model = Calculation extra = 0 raw_id_fields = ('accepted_price', 'created_by') class OfferInline(admin.StackedInline): model = Offer extra = 0 raw_id_fields = ('contract', 'subsidy', 'created_by', 'package', 'shopping_cart_package') class PackagePriceInline(admin.TabularInline): model = PackagePrice extra = 0 readonly_fields = ('price', 'package') can_delete = False class ShoppingCartPackageInline(admin.TabularInline): model = ShoppingCartPackage extra = 0 readonly_fields = ('shopping_cart', 'package') can_delete = False @admin.register(Calculation) class CalculationAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin): date_hierarchy = 'created_at' fieldsets = ( ( None, { 'fields': ( 'interest', 'created_by', 'expire_date', 'num_views', 'accepted', 'accepted_price', ) }, ), ( 'Pricing', { 'fields': ( 'cost_model', 'price_model', 'price_error', 'discount_link', ) }, ), ) list_display = ('__str__', 'created_by') list_filter = (('created_by', admin.RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter),) list_select_related = ('interest', 'created_by') raw_id_fields = ('accepted_price', 'interest') readonly_fields = ('created_at', 'created_by', 'discount_link') search_fields = ( 'interest__unclean_address', 'interest__email', 'interest__phone_number', ) inlines = (PackagePriceInline, ShoppingCartPackageInline, OfferInline) def discount_link(self, obj): if not obj.discount: return '-' link = reverse( 'admin:discounts_discount_change', args=[obj.discount.id], urlconf='cloud.urls', ) return mark_safe(f'{obj.discount}') def get_queryset(self, request): return ( super() .get_queryset(request) .select_related('interest__address__municipality', 'interest__address__zip_code') ) @admin.register(Offer) class OfferAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin): date_hierarchy = 'created_at' list_display = ('__str__', 'created_by') list_filter = (('created_by', admin.RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter),) list_select_related = ( 'created_by', 'calculation__interest__address__zip_code', 'calculation__interest__address__municipality__county', ) raw_id_fields = ('calculation', 'created_by', 'contract', 'package') readonly_fields = ('created_at',) search_fields = ( 'calculation__interest__unclean_address', 'calculation__interest__email', 'calculation__interest__phone_number', ) class FileInline(admin.StackedInline): model = File extra = 0 raw_id_fields = ('calculation', 'user') @admin.register(File) class FileAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin): date_hierarchy = 'timestamp' list_display = ('timestamp', 'user', 'file', 'filename') list_filter = ('mime_type', 'aerial') raw_id_fields = ('interest', 'calculation', 'user') search_fields = ( 'interest__unclean_address', 'interest__email', 'interest__phone_number', ) class UserInputInline(admin.StackedInline): model = UserInput can_delete = False max_num = 1 min_num = 0 extra = 0 classes = ('collapse',) fieldsets = ( ( 'Basic Information', { 'fields': ( 'intention_to_buy', 'interesting_purchase_models', 'property_ownership_status', ) }, ), ( 'Energy Information', { 'fields': ( 'monthly_energy_spend', 'monthly_energy_consumption', 'yearly_energy_consumption', 'yearly_energy_spend', 'yearly_consumption_kwh', 'monthly_energy_bill', ) }, ), ( 'Installation Details', { 'fields': ( 'installation_placement', 'planning_permission', 'type_of_area', 'roof_slope', 'roof_frame', 'eave_height', 'mounting_surface', 'surface', ) }, ), ( 'Technical Details', { 'fields': ( 'grid_phase', 'standard_electrical_cabinet', 'special_circumstances', 'asbestos_in_roof', ) }, ), ( 'Other', { 'fields': ( 'preferred_subsidy', 'consent_to_share_data_with_partner', 'customer_input', ) }, ), ) raw_id_fields = ('mounting_surface', 'surface') @admin.register(Interest) class InterestAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin): date_hierarchy = 'created_at' inlines = (CalculationInline, FileInline, UserInputInline) list_display = ( 'created_at', 'activated_at', 'won_at', 'address', 'email', 'phone_number', 'customer', 'lead_qualifier', 'agent', 'status', ) list_filter = ( ('lead_qualifier', admin.RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter), ('agent', admin.RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter), 'status', 'experiment_version', 'calculation_engine', ) list_select_related = ( 'address__municipality__county', 'address__zip_code', 'customer', 'lead_qualifier', 'agent', ) raw_id_fields = ( 'address', 'lead_qualifier', 'agent', 'customer', 'user', 'preferred_surface', 'affiliate_campaign', 'current_shopping_cart', ) readonly_fields = ( 'created_at', 'updated_at', 'activated_at', 'source_type', ) exclude = ('lead',) search_fields = ('unclean_address', 'email', 'phone_number') ``` I'll help reorganize the admin fieldsets to group related information logically and prioritize the most important information. Here's my suggested reorganization: ```python @admin.register(Interest) class InterestAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin): date_hierarchy = 'created_at' inlines = (CalculationInline, FileInline, UserInputInline) fieldsets = ( ( 'Core Information', { 'fields': ( 'status', 'email', 'phone_number', 'address', 'unclean_address', 'customer', ) }, ), ( 'Assignment', { 'fields': ( 'lead_qualifier', 'agent', 'user', ) }, ), ( 'Dates', { 'fields': ( 'created_at', 'updated_at', 'activated_at', 'won_at', ) }, ), ( 'Technical Settings', { 'classes': ('collapse',), 'fields': ( 'calculation_engine', 'preferred_surface', 'current_shopping_cart', ) }, ), ( 'Marketing', { 'classes': ('collapse',), 'fields': ( 'source_type', 'affiliate_campaign', 'experiment_version', ) }, ), ) @admin.register(Calculation) class CalculationAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin): fieldsets = ( ( 'Basic Information', { 'fields': ( 'interest', 'created_by', 'expire_date', ) }, ), ( 'Status', { 'fields': ( 'accepted', 'accepted_price', 'num_views', ) }, ), ( 'Pricing Details', { 'classes': ('collapse',), 'fields': ( 'cost_model', 'price_model', 'price_error', 'discount_link', ) }, ), ) @admin.register(File) class FileAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin): fieldsets = ( ( 'File Information', { 'fields': ( 'file', 'filename', 'mime_type', 'timestamp', ) }, ), ( 'Relations', { 'fields': ( 'interest', 'calculation', 'user', ) }, ), ( 'Properties', { 'classes': ('collapse',), 'fields': ( 'aerial', ) }, ), ) @admin.register(Offer) class OfferAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin): fieldsets = ( ( 'Basic Information', { 'fields': ( 'calculation', 'created_by', 'created_at', ) }, ), ( 'Package Details', { 'fields': ( 'package', 'shopping_cart_package', ) }, ), ( 'Contract Information', { 'classes': ('collapse',), 'fields': ( 'contract', 'subsidy', ) }, ), ) ``` I've organized the fieldsets with these principles: 1. Most important/frequently used fields are at the top and always visible 2. Related fields are grouped together logically 3. Less frequently used or technical fields are collapsed by default 4. Clear, descriptive section names 5. Core information and status fields are prominently displayed 6. Technical/advanced settings are tucked away in collapsed sections The organization focuses on: - Making the most important information immediately visible - Reducing visual clutter - Creating a logical flow of information - Making it easier to find specific fields - Separating primary from secondary information This should make the admin interface more user-friendly while maintaining access to all functionality. 'created_at' cannot be specified for Interest model form as it is a non-editable field. Check fields/fieldsets/exclude attributes of class InterestAdmin. Ah yes, my apologies. We need to remove `created_at`, `updated_at`, and other readonly fields from the fieldsets since they're specified in `readonly_fields`. Here's the corrected version of the InterestAdmin: ```python @admin.register(Interest) class InterestAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin): date_hierarchy = 'created_at' inlines = (CalculationInline, FileInline, UserInputInline) readonly_fields = ( 'created_at', 'updated_at', 'activated_at', 'source_type', ) fieldsets = ( ( 'Core Information', { 'fields': ( 'status', 'email', 'phone_number', 'address', 'unclean_address', 'customer', ) }, ), ( 'Assignment', { 'fields': ( 'lead_qualifier', 'agent', 'user', ) }, ), ( 'Timestamps', { 'fields': ( 'created_at', # readonly 'updated_at', # readonly 'activated_at', # readonly 'won_at', ) }, ), ( 'Technical Settings', { 'classes': ('collapse',), 'fields': ( 'calculation_engine', 'preferred_surface', 'current_shopping_cart', ) }, ), ( 'Marketing', { 'classes': ('collapse',), 'fields': ( 'source_type', # readonly 'affiliate_campaign', 'experiment_version', ) }, ), ) list_display = ( 'created_at', 'activated_at', 'won_at', 'address', 'email', 'phone_number', 'customer', 'lead_qualifier', 'agent', 'status', ) list_filter = ( ('lead_qualifier', admin.RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter), ('agent', admin.RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter), 'status', 'experiment_version', 'calculation_engine', ) list_select_related = ( 'address__municipality__county', 'address__zip_code', 'customer', 'lead_qualifier', 'agent', ) raw_id_fields = ( 'address', 'lead_qualifier', 'agent', 'customer', 'user', 'preferred_surface', 'affiliate_campaign', 'current_shopping_cart', ) exclude = ('lead',) search_fields = ('unclean_address', 'email', 'phone_number') ``` The readonly fields are now properly included in both the `readonly_fields` attribute and the fieldsets. This will display them in the form but prevent them from being edited. The rest of the organization remains the same, maintaining the logical grouping and progressive disclosure of information.
